steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence describes a relationship between a long-held scholarly view (silk first spread beyond China in the second century CE) and new archaeological evidence (the Indus Civilization used silk at least 1,000 years earlier). The blank must describe what the evidence has done to that view. The evidence directly contradicts it — a 1,000-year gap means the old view is wrong.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'investigated': “Investigated” means studied or examined. Evidence can investigate a question, but it cannot investigate a view in this sense. More importantly, it says nothing about the view being overturned. ✗
  • Choice B — 'contradicted': “Contradicted” means shown to be false or wrong. The evidence places silk use 1,000 years earlier than scholars believed — directly disproving the accepted view. ✓
  • Choice C — 'misinterpreted': “Misinterpreted” means understood incorrectly. Evidence doesn’t misinterpret a view; people do. The word doesn’t fit the sentence logically. ✗
  • Choice D — 'anticipated': “Anticipated” means expected or predicted in advance. The new evidence came after the scholars’ view, so it cannot have anticipated it. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
